title: Constipation - self-care
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Constipation can mean stool is less frequent than normal, hard, dry, or difficult to pass.
Self-care concepts include regular activity, drinking water, adding fiber gradually, responding to the urge to stool, and bowel routine training.
Laxative use should be clinician-directed, time-limited, and avoided when severe abdominal pain, nausea, or vomiting is present.
Patients should contact a clinician for no bowel movement for several days, abdominal bloating or pain, nausea or vomiting, or blood in stool.
